prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, the scribes of the ledger speak of "Annual Recurring Revenue" as if it were a harvest that returns each season without seed or plough, and lo, it hath swelled to the number of ten thousand silver marks. A great pestilence they call "churn" did claim a portion of the faithful user-base, who fled as if from a dragon, though no beast was observed.

In this quarter, the company did embark upon a crusade termed "Series B," not against any infidel, but within the glittering courts of venture capitalists, securing a chest of gold so vast it is said to have "extended the runway," a phrase which conjures images of a very long carpet for a flying machine that doth not exist.

They celebrate the "net retention" exceeding one hundred and ten percent, a most bewildering arithmetic where one keeps not only all of one's sheep, but also receives new sheep from the very flock that was already thine own.

Behold, the Grand Chamberlain of Product did unveil a new feature named "Synergy," a silent and invisible engine that, when placed within the main castle, is said to make all the other engines work harder whilst asleep.
